# Jenkins Design Library

> A reference library of UI components and patterns for building Jenkins plugin interfaces.

For complete documentation of all components with code examples, see [llms-all.txt](./llms-all.txt).

## Components
- [App bars](./app-bars.md): Frames your page and contains your most important actions.
- [Banner](./banner.md): Banners are a handy way to display small snippets of text and actions.
- [Buttons](./buttons.md): Triggers specific actions with a click or tap.
- [Cards](./cards.md): Use cards to surface related information and controls to users.
- [Checkboxes](./checkboxes.md): Allows users to select one or more items from a list.
- [Dialogs](./dialogs.md): Displays overlay windows for additional information or user input without navigating away.
- [Empty States](./empty-states.md): For when there is no data, no configuration or no plugins installed that support the required feature an empty state can be provided instead.
- [File](./file.md): Enables file upload functionality.
- [Inputs](./inputs.md): Captures user input through various text or data entry formats.
- [Menu](./menu.md): Menus allow you to group similar controls under one roof. They're an effective way to de-clutter your page whilst offering users the actions they need.
- [Notifications](./notifications.md): Jenkins can display in-page notifications with a simple-to-use JavaScript API.
- [Progress](./progress.md): Indicates the completion status of a task or operation.
- [Radios](./radios.md): Allows users to select a single option from a group.
- [Repeatable list](./repeatable-list.md): Displays lists with varying content types within the same container.
- [Select](./select.md): Provides a dropdown for choosing one option from a predefined list.
- [Symbols](./symbols.md): Jenkins Symbols are an extensive and consistent collection of icons for use in Jenkins and plugins.
- [Table](./table.md): Displays structured data in rows and columns, with support for sorting and badges.
- [Toggle switch](./toggle-switch.md): Enables users to switch between two states, such as on or off.
- [Tooltips](./tooltips.md): Offers brief, contextual information when hovering over an element.

## Patterns
- [Colors](./colors.md): Defines the palette for consistent use of color.
- [JavaScript Proxy](./javascript-proxy.md): Export arbitrary server-side Java object to JavaScript and invoke their methods from JavaScript.
- [Layouts](./layouts.md): Predefined layout structures and patterns to organize page content.
- [Links](./links.md): Creates navigational connections to internal or external destinations.
- [Localization](./localization.md): Ensures texts can be displayed in the user's language.
- [Spacing](./spacing.md): Defines consistent padding, margins, and gaps between elements.
- [Stylesheets](./stylesheets.md): Provides reusable stylesheet principles and standards for maintaining consistency.
- [Validation](./validation.md): Ensures user inputs meet specified criteria or rules before submission.
